questions.text: The meristem present at the tips of roots and shoots is:
questions.explanation: Apical meristem is present at the growing tips of roots and shoots and is responsible for primary growth.

questions.text: Xylem conducts water and minerals from roots to leaves.
questions.explanation: Xylem is the water-conducting tissue that transports water and minerals from roots to other parts of the plant.

questions.text: The tissue that conducts food in plants is ________.
questions.explanation: Phloem is the food-conducting tissue that transports organic nutrients (mainly sucrose) from leaves to other parts.

questions.correctAnswer: Stomata are tiny pores in the epidermis of leaves that regulate gas exchange and transpiration.
questions.explanation: Stomata are surrounded by guard cells and help in gas exchange (CO2 intake, O2 release) and transpiration.
